raw_input:
[3.0/5] I Don't Know How I Feel About This Pros:

Very cute and compact facial massager in the shape of a lipstick tube. It comes with a USB cable. The massager charges very quickly for immediate use.

Cons:

I feel that the massager is a little weak to actually work. I feel most of the vibration at the base of the product, and not much at the tip. Also, the heating element of this product is very weak. It barely gets warm. [4.0/5] After a month of use I can see a visible difference I use this every day. While I'm brushing my teeth and getting ready for my day. It cane with no instructions but its fairly easy to figure out. Too, just come back to the product description online and read how to use it. Charged pretty quick and lasts about 4 days on one charge. The laugh lines and frown lines are slowly loosing their depth and overall, my skin tone has evened out quite a bit. I'm happy with it. Its quick, very convenient and does offer results with disciplined use. Try it!! [5.0/5] Helps with dark circles and wrinkles I have dark puffy circles under my eyes and I wanted something that would help with getting my eye moisturizer to really soak in to my skin and this does just that.
After I wash my face I'll add eye serum and use this doing exactly what the instructions say to do and it really feels good the heat feels relaxing around my eyes and my dark circles seem to be disappearing and I even use it on my face and my face looks brighter so far and seems to be less wrinkles. This product really works for me and I use it regularly. Good value for the money. [5.0/5] Interesting device This device is very thoughtful, and has just about everything you need to massage under and around you eyes - or any area that needs attention on the face. Very gentle. [3.0/5] Cute - works OK - doesn’t get too warm…No directions This eye massage thing doesn’t come with any directions. Now it’s not rocket science but I would like to know if there’s supposed to be heat in each function - if it supposed to pulse on the blue and the red light etc. I say this because I think there’s only heat when the red light is on. The rest seem to have no heat. And it is very light - very slight heat as it is. It makes me question is there even heat or has my face heated the crystal up. So I was really expecting it to have much more heat. Also with both the red and the blue light it’s not a constant vibration - it’s kind of like a pulse vibration it goes on and it goes off then it goes on and it goes off.

I do like that you can use it without the red or the blue light because the third setting is just a vibration - and that one is a constant vibration. But again that setting seems to have no heat at all.

The crystal does seem to be some kind of quartz. It is a clear type of crystal so I assume it’s quartz because quartz is pretty common.

It feels pretty nice around my eyes and if you hold it a certain way the red and/or or blue light will not get into your eye - you won’t see it while you use it. And I do like that. So this is OK and it looks just like a tube of lipstick. [5.0/5] Refreshing There were no directions so I wasn’t quite sure what to do. I’ve been applying it often but haven’t really seen any difference. [3.0/5] On the fence… I’m still on the fence about this product. It doesn’t come with any directions but, because I’m basically an expert user in red light therapy devices I know how to use this. Someone who is not though, may not be sure…?
The component itself just feels kind of cheap. It’s not a ‘real’ stone. It doesn’t heat up very well. And although it charges super quickly, I just don’t think this device is strong enough to be helpful to the under eyes. I don’t think it’s hurting anything. But, I don’t think it’s helping in any way either, unfortunately.
It is very cute though. [2.0/5] Heat feels nice, but otherwise not impressed. I wanted to like this product, but overall I'm not particularly impressed with it. The lipstick design is cute, but the entire device is covered in a cheap hard plastic that attracts dust and smudges, and doesn't feel particularly durable. The gemstone tip looks dull and cloudy, and has bubbles within it. Not a pretty pink rose quartz like in the product photos. The stone also doesn't feel perfectly smooth, but somewhat coarse or tacky.

I'm not really certain what the difference between the red and blue light modes is; They both seem to head the gemstone head. The 'button' being a touch sensor makes it easy to switch modes on accidental during use. Perhaps one of the biggest flaws of this device is that the motor is in the base, and the vibrations are extremely weak. This means that you can barely feel the vibrations at all during use. Additionally, the charging port is completely exposed on the bottom of this device with no water-resistant cover, so there's the potentially to accidentally get liquid on it if using this product to encourage product absorption.

Overall, I'm highly disappointed with this product. I think it's a really cool idea, and the heat emitted from the stone feels nice, but I feel like the overall execution of this product is poorly done. [5.0/5] Heating and vibrations feel good on my face Seemed like good quality. Packaging was impressive. Felt good on the skin and the warmth was very pleasant Time will tell if good results with this [2.0/5] I don’t get it This thing sucks, it has zero power and doesn’t even heat up and get warm. It’s like massaging a rock on your face, and idk why it looks like lipstick. I’d choose something else if you’re looking for an eye massager. [2.0/5] Vibration is not very strong I'm giving this eye massager 2 stars for its design alone. I really liked the lipstick shape. It was easy to massage my under eye. That is about the only thing I liked about it.
The rose quartz feels more like cheap plastic. Vibration is not very strong. Doesn't get very warm. [1.0/5] Cheaply made, not real quartz stone I think this product has been falsely advertised and the stone is not quartz. It is made and feels like cheap plastic. The heating and massage is very weak and can barely be felt. Overall, not a good product and package is not even sealed.

What I like:
None.

Concerns:
1. Not quartz
2. Weak if any massage function
3. Cheaply made
4. Not sealed package for a hygiene product [2.0/5] Doesn’t really do anything It’s a cute little gadget I’ve tried it both by itself and to apply facial serum and I really see no noticeable effects. It doesn’t stay charged well and the stone doesn’t really seem like a stone. [3.0/5] No directions and not very effective There were not any directions with this. It's not rocket science; but, directions are always nice so you at least know you're doing it right.

Overall this was not very effective for me. I was disappointed. [5.0/5] Great eye massager In love with this
Works great
I use it with my serums
Has 3 modes
Very relaxing
Durable
Value
And comes with a charger [3.0/5] Good sensation but not worth the time or money [[VIDEOID:cf747606acf9b5900867c48bd6720d4]] This under eye massager comes in a conveniently small package that can be easily stored. It comes with three massage settings. The first is the red light which warms the massage stone and provides a pulsating massage. The second is the blue light which cools the massage stone and provides a pulsating massage. The Third leaves the stone at room temperature with no light indicator, and provides a constant massage vibration. Hall of the massage settings feel nice on your under eye area, but I didn’t find that they helped in absorption of my under eye creams, nor did they help with any under eye discoloration or puffiness. So though the massage tool felt nice on my under eye area, I didn’t find any effectiveness that would necessitate use of this on a regular basis. [5.0/5] Eye Massage Great eye massage!! Comes in handy when your on the go!! I like how it looks like lipstick!! Massage my eye good!! Love it [5.0/5] Cute little device This review is for a MEIQIER Eye Massager with Natural Quartz Jade. I ordered this to replace a similar device that didn't have a Quartz head or blue light(just red). This device has a Rose Quartz tip, heat, massage, red light(collagen production) and blue light(calms inflammation) therapy. It uses USB charging. The eye massager comes with a protective tip cap/cover and looks like a cute lipstick.
I like to use first the red light, heat, massage with my eye serum and then I use blue light, heat massage, with my eyecream. I am using this device only on my eyes(I have full face device with a larger head). It feels very soothing on my eyes and helps with puffiness too. Since it is small and has a cap, it is easy to just toss it in a purse on the go. This device would make a great gift! [4.0/5] More novelty than productive This has a fun design, masquerading as lipstick. However, it when it comes to efficacy, it falls short for me. The vibration and heat are weak compared to other facial devices. It is mostly made of lightweight plastic, so I'm not sure the quality is there or the lifespan.

response:
Here is a summary of the product reviews:

This facial massager device, which resembles a lipstick tube, receives mixed reviews overall. On the positive side, many users appreciate its cute and compact design, quick charging, and the pleasant sensation of warmth and vibration around the eye area. Some users report visible improvements in dark circles, puffiness, fine lines, and skin tone with regular use. The device has multiple modes including red and blue light settings, and comes with a USB charging cable that provides several days of battery life.

However, there are several common complaints. Many reviewers feel the vibration and heating functions are too weak to be truly effective. The rose quartz or crystal tip is frequently criticized as feeling like cheap plastic rather than real stone, with some reviewers believing it is falsely advertised. A lack of clear instructions is another recurring issue, leaving users unsure how to properly use the different settings. Some also note the device feels cheaply made, attracts dust and smudges, and may not be durable long term.

Overall, customers seem divided. Those who enjoy it tend to use it as part of a daily skincare routine to help serums absorb into the skin. Those who are disappointed feel the device underdelivers on its promises and question the quality of materials. It appears best suited for someone looking for a gentle, relaxing addition to their skincare routine rather than a powerful treatment tool.
